title: Middle East oil/rates shock can affect new-buy timing
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Jun 3 FlashInsights / Daily Technical Strategy
summary: Fundstrat technical work flagged a near-term WTI and 10-year yield bounce tied to Gulf/Iran-war headlines before a possible reversal; review exposure before new risk adds.
trigger: WTI approaches 99-101, 10Y yield approaches roughly 4.55-4.59, or Strait/ceasefire headlines change abruptly.

title: Financials breakdown and crypto-linked weakness weigh on tape breadth
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Daily Technical Strategy
summary: Fundstrat technical work flagged equal-weight financial weakness and crypto-linked financial pressure as a tape negative that may persist over coming weeks.
trigger: RYF remains below support near 74.40, crypto-linked financials fail to stabilize, or XLF/RYF relative weakness accelerates.

title: Narrow AI leadership versus improving equal-weight participation
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Mark Newton webinar notice
summary: Fundstrat noted narrow semiconductor/AI leadership, oil swings, and a potential breadth shift as a rotation risk to monitor rather than an all-clear tape read.
trigger: Mag 7/AI leadership weakens while equal-weight participation improves, or oil swings disrupt growth leadership.

title: US-Iran framework deal cuts immediate oil shock but Hormuz normalization stays unresolved
source: 2026-06-15 public reporting on U.S.-Iran framework deal; Polymarket Hormuz Jun 30 market ~30% yes; UW market tide 2026-06-15 remained net call-premium positive
summary: De-escalation headlines and strong same-session broad tape lower the odds that the June 3 oil/rates shock should be treated as unchanged tomorrow, but unresolved shipping normalization means the event-risk lane should stay active rather than clear.
trigger: Invalidate the de-escalation read if Hormuz/shipping restrictions return, peace-deal terms break down, or WTI/TNX re-spike; upgrade only if official terms and unrestricted shipping are confirmed.

title: Hormuz peace odds improve but traffic normalization still not cleared
source: Polymarket Gamma/public page checked 2026-06-17T01:25Z; Guardian live report 2026-06-16 17:19 ET
summary: US-Iran peace-by-June odds were near-certain while the Strait of Hormuz normal-by-June market was only about 17.5-18% Yes; de-escalation lowers immediate war/oil shock but shipping normalization remains unconfirmed.
trigger: Keep event-risk lane active until IMF PortWatch transit calls recover, WTI/TNX stay lower, and shipping/insurance friction fades; invalidate relief if deal terms break or Hormuz restrictions return.

title: Hormuz shifts from June miss to July normalization watch
source: Polymarket/Kalshi public Hormuz markets checked 2026-06-18T01:22Z; Guardian 2026-06-17 MOU report
summary: Prediction markets and public reporting now point to lower acute war/oil shock but unresolved traffic normalization: Polymarket Jun30 Yes ~24%, Jul31 Yes ~55%, Kalshi Jul15 ~33% and Aug1 ~52%; Guardian reports 30-day restoration target with 60-day final-deal negotiation and walk-away risk.
trigger: Do not clear the event-risk lane until IMF PortWatch 7-day transit calls recover above the market threshold, WTI/TNX stay lower, and shipping/insurance friction fades; re-escalate if deal terms break, toll/access conditions return, or Hormuz restrictions resume.

title: Hormuz recovery evidence keeps oil/rates normalization unproven
source: Guardian 2026-06-19; Business Insider/Kpler 2026-06-19; Kalshi KXHORMUZNORM public API fetched 2026-06-20T01:24Z
summary: Guardian reports the center route remains blocked by roughly 80 mines and normal shipping may take time despite the US-Iran MoU; Kpler/BI report 25 verified crossings on June 18, so recovery is real but incomplete; Kalshi still prices PortWatch normalization above 60 transit-call 7DMA at about 8% by Jul 1, 20% by Jul 15, 40% by Aug 1, 56% by Sep 1, and 64% by Oct 1. This keeps oil/rates event risk active for new-buy timing instead of clearing the shock.
trigger: Clear only if IMF PortWatch 7DMA rises above 60, WTI/TNX stay below trigger bands, and mine/toll/insurance/shipping friction fades; re-escalate if the MoU breaks, crossings roll over, or toll/access restrictions return.
